source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure AWS Management Console authentication failures are monitored

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure AWS Management Console authentication failures are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for failed console authentication attempts.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring failed console logins may decrease the lead time to detect an attempt to brute-force a credential, which may provide an indicator, such as the source IP address, that can be used in other event correlations. Impact: Monitoring for these failures may generate a large number of alerts, especially in larger environments.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group- name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = ConsoleLogin) && ($.errorMessage = "Failed authentication") }" 4. Note the <console-signin-failure-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <console-signin-failure- metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<console-signin-failure-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for AWS management Console login failures and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> -- filter-name <console-signin-failure-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<console-signin-failure- metric>,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = ConsoleLogin) && ($.errorMessage = "Failed authentication") }' Note : You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note : Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <console-signin-failure- alarm> --metric-name <console-signin-failure-metric> --statistic Sum -- period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn> References: 1. CCE-79191-3 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored
input_row.impact: Monitoring for these failures may generate a large number of alerts, especially in larger environments.
input_row.references: 1. CCE-79191-3 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** 

The control to ensure AWS Management Console authentication failures are monitored can be fully verified using AWS APIs and CLI commands. The detailed audit procedures specify step-by-step verification processes, including using AWS CLI commands such as `describe-trails`, `get-trail-status`, `get-event-selectors`, `describe-metric-filters`, `describe-alarms`, and `list-subscriptions-by-topic`. These commands check for the existence and configuration of CloudTrail, CloudWatch metric filters, and SNS subscriptions. The automation possibilities align with standard AWS services and their properties related to logging and monitoring which are exposed via AWS APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_console_authentication_failures_monitored

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **AWS Services**: CloudTrail, CloudWatch, SNS
- **AWS API Calls**:
  - **CloudTrail**: Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()` to identify multi-region trails and ensure `IsMultiRegionTrail` is true. Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status(Name=trail_name)` to verify `IsLogging` is true, and `get_event_selectors(trail_name=trail_name)` to ensure it captures all management events.
  - **CloudWatch Logs**: Use `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=log_group_name)` to ensure the metric filter pattern `{ ($.eventName = ConsoleLogin) && ($.errorMessage = "Failed authentication") }` is present.
  - **CloudWatch Alarms**: Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()` to check for alarms based on the metric filter and verify they have alarm actions specified.
  - **SNS**: Use `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn=topic_arn)` to ensure there is an active subscription.
- **Verification**:
  - Ensure there is an active multi-region CloudTrail logging management events.
  - Validate CloudWatch Logs contain a metric filter that includes the specified filter pattern.
  - Confirm a CloudWatch alarm is configured with appropriate SNS topics and has active subscriptions.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria**: 
  - **Pass**: All components (CloudTrail active, metric filter configured, alarm set up with SNS subscription) are correctly configured.
  - **Fail**: Any component is missing or misconfigured (e.g., missing metric filter, alarm not tied to SNS).

This automated approach ensures comprehensive and consistent monitoring of AWS Management Console authentication failures, reducing manual effort and increasing reliability of compliance verification.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively verified using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI commands. The use of these tools aligns with the capabilities provided by AWS for monitoring and logging services, such as CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The boto3 methods and CLI commands mentioned are valid for the tasks described. However, there are minor improvements that could be made for completeness and accuracy.
- The program name `aws_cloudtrail_console_authentication_failures_monitored` is descriptive and clear, but it could be slightly improved for clarity and convention adherence.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- **Metric Filter Pattern:** The pattern `{ ($.eventName = ConsoleLogin) && ($.errorMessage = "Failed authentication") }` is a good start, but it should be verified against the actual structure of CloudTrail logs to ensure it captures all relevant failed login attempts. Consider using a more comprehensive pattern that accounts for variations in the error message.
- **CloudWatch Alarm Verification:** Ensure that the alarm is not only configured but also actively monitoring the metric filter. Check for alarm states and recent activities to confirm operational status.
- **SNS Subscription Verification:** While checking for active subscriptions is good, ensure that the SNS topic is correctly integrated with the CloudWatch alarm actions. Consider verifying the endpoint types (e.g., email, Lambda) to ensure they are appropriate for alerting.
- **Alternative Methods:** Consider using AWS Config rules to provide additional compliance checks. AWS Config can continuously monitor and assess the configuration of AWS resources.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** Consider renaming to `aws_cloudtrail_console_login_failures_monitoring` for better clarity and alignment with naming conventions.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-structured, and the use of AWS services and APIs is appropriate for the control. The technical details are mostly accurate, with only minor areas for improvement.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify
- Enhance the metric filter pattern for completeness.
- Verify the operational status of CloudWatch alarms.
- Ensure SNS topic integration is thoroughly checked.
- Consider renaming the program for better clarity and convention adherence.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that all required audit steps—verifying CloudTrail multi-region trails, CloudWatch metric filters, alarms, and SNS subscriptions—can be performed using AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). All relevant configuration and status data are exposed via AWS SDKs, making full automation feasible and reliable for this control.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_cloudtrail_log_group_console_auth_failure_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** CloudTrail Trails, CloudWatch Log Groups, CloudWatch Metric Filters, CloudWatch Alarms, SNS Topics/Subscriptions
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - CloudTrail: `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()`, `get_trail_status()`, `get_event_selectors()`
  - CloudWatch Logs: `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters()`
  - CloudWatch Alarms: `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()`
  - SNS: `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic()`
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - At least one multi-region CloudTrail trail with `IsMultiRegionTrail=True` and `IsLogging=True`
  - Trail is logging management events (`ReadWriteType=All` or includes `ConsoleLogin`)
  - CloudWatch Log Group attached to the trail
  - Metric filter on the log group with a pattern matching failed console authentication attempts (e.g., `{ ($.eventName = ConsoleLogin) && ($.errorMessage = "Failed authentication") }`)
  - CloudWatch Alarm configured on the above metric filter and in `OK` or `ALARM` state
  - SNS topic with at least one confirmed subscription as the alarm action
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - All above properties are present and correctly configured
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - Any required property is missing, misconfigured, or not active (e.g., no multi-region trail, missing metric filter, alarm not present or not linked to SNS, etc.)

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no substantive disagreements; both AI opinions supported automation. The second opinion suggested minor improvements to metric filter accuracy and alarm/SNS integration, which are incorporated in the detailed automation criteria.

**SOURCE:** CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
